title: Accessibility audit (contrast ratios + colour-blind simulation) across the set
context: The automated verification harness checks contrast ratios and basic colour-blind safety, but a manual accessibility audit provides a deeper review: testing with actual colour-blind simulation tools, checking that the large-print variant is adequate for low-vision use in practice, and identifying any edge cases the automated checks missed. The audit findings feed directly back into the style guide and design system as concrete change requests.
objective: Conduct a comprehensive accessibility audit of the full core set (all four families, all variants) covering contrast ratios, colour-blind simulation, and large-print adequacy, producing actionable findings.
acceptanceCriteria: Confirms text/numeral contrast meets WCAG AA (>= 4.5:1) for all colour and grayscale variants across all four families.
Colour-blind simulation (protanopia, deuteranopia, tritanopia) confirms no information is lost without colour for any family in any variant.
Large-print variant is evaluated for adequacy for low-vision use by a qualified accessibility reviewer; gaps are logged as specific, actionable tasks.
Ink-saver outline variant is evaluated: patterns/hatching are distinguishable in black-and-white print.
Audit report lists all findings with pass/fail status and any required follow-up tasks, published CC-BY-4.0.
resources: tasks/math-manip-data-002.json -- full core set to audit
output: An accessibility audit report (docs/accessibility-audit.md) with per-family, per-variant findings and a list of any follow-up tasks, CC-BY-4.0.
